patch.value:
## Context
PR#617 (pm-pibw) made id entropy configurable and its migration guidance explicitly recommends `ids.token_length 6` for multi-agent/branch repositories. This repository is the flagship multi-agent fan-out workspace (parallel maintainer/codex/pi lanes branching between merges) yet still runs the default token_length 4 (~184 concurrent unsynced creations for a 1% birthday collision). pm-iwsj adopted the merge-safety contract but did not cover entropy.

## Change
`pm config set project ids.token_length 6` (2.18B id space, ~6.6k concurrent unsynced creations at 1%). Existing 4-char ids stay valid; only newly minted ids grow.
Adopt collision-resistant id entropy in the pm-cli repository (ids.token_length 4 -> 6)
settings.json carries ids.token_length 6; pm config project get ids_token_length reports 6; guidance rationale recorded on the item

author: maintainer-agent

patch.value: Adopted ids.token_length 6 in the repository settings.json as a minimal 3-line sparse diff (full-default materialization from the config-set round-trip deliberately reverted and filed as pm-x2aplf); rationale and birthday-bound table live in MERGE_SAFETY.md.
Newly minted item ids carry 6-char base36 tokens (~2.18B id space), bounding cross-branch collision risk for the multi-agent lane workflow; existing 4-char ids remain valid.
pm config project get ids_token_length reports 6; first minted items pm-46octv and pm-x2aplf carry 6-char tokens; validate storage_integrity ok.

Adopted ids.token_length 6 in the repository settings.json as a minimal 3-line sparse diff (full-default materialization from the config-set round-trip deliberately reverted and filed as pm-x2aplf); rationale and birthday-bound table live in MERGE_SAFETY.md.

message: Applied sparse-diff adoption; materialization side effect reverted and tracked separately as pm-x2aplf.
